scenario.title: Claim Following iPhone Recovery After Suspected Theft With Mixed Damage Timeline
scenario.description: Customer reported their iPhone lost under unclear circumstances, with suspicion of theft but no official police report filed or device lock flagged. Several days later, the device was found by a third party in a public location and returned to the customer, who resumed regular use. Within a week, the device began manifesting issues such as unresponsive touch input, ghost touches, and reduced camera functionality. Inspection revealed faint water indicator coloration and minor corrosion near the SIM tray, but otherwise the device appeared intact. The customer states that the device was fully functional and dry both before the presumed loss and upon its return, only later exhibiting symptoms. The customer seeks coverage for accidental damage from handling (ADH) under AppleCare+.

service_fee.notes: ADH fee may apply if claim accepted as accidental damage rather than a loss/theft exclusion; requires assessment of when and how damage occurred.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to a Covered iPhone that has been lost or stolen; coverage only applies to a device that is returned to Apple in its entirety.
ADH coverage applies to operational or mechanical failures caused by an unexpected and unintentional external event on a device returned intact.

scenario.reasoning: No police report or device-blacklist means a precise 'lost or stolen' classification is not established, just suspected. The iPhone was found intact, making it arguably 'returned in its entirety.' Damage timing is ambiguous—symptoms only appeared after return, resembling covered accidental damage. However, concurrent signs of prior exposure and unexplained absence mean the cause and coverage eligibility require careful determination. This sits between theft/loss exclusion and routine ADH coverage.
scenario.adjustments: Changed scenario from clearly reported and documented theft with recovery to an ambiguous loss with no police report, no device lock, and no missing components; added a credible timeline for post-return damage symptoms, with device returned by a third party; introduced unclear cause of device issues and faint water/corrosion indicators to muddle the damage timing.
scenario.risk_analysis: The scenario now balances between a (potentially) covered ADH claim—since the device is returned intact and damage appears after return—and a not covered loss/theft claim due to ambiguous disappearance and possible adverse events during the missing period. The lack of formal theft confirmation, as well as questions about the moment and cause of damage, create genuine ambiguity regarding AppleCare+ coverage eligibility under Sections 3.2 and 4.2(iv) and would likely prompt an in-depth investigation.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private for-profit company or business, or of an individual, for wages, salary, or commissions
persona.detailed job description: Provided administrative support in a legal firm
persona.education: 1 or more years of college credit, no degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.occupation category: OFF-Secretaries And Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, And Executive
persona.personal time: Spends free time in nature or reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Claim Following iPhone Recovery After Suspected Theft With Mixed Damage Timeline

Scenario description:
Customer reported their iPhone lost under unclear circumstances, with suspicion of theft but no official police report filed or device lock flagged. Several days later, the device was found by a third party in a public location and returned to the customer, who resumed regular use. Within a week, the device began manifesting issues such as unresponsive touch input, ghost touches, and reduced camera functionality. Inspection revealed faint water indicator coloration and minor corrosion near the SIM tray, but otherwise the device appeared intact. The customer states that the device was fully functional and dry both before the presumed loss and upon its return, only later exhibiting symptoms. The customer seeks coverage for accidental damage from handling (ADH) under AppleCare+.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi, I need to file a claim. My iPhone was stolen, then it was found a few days later, but now it's acting up—touchscreen's wonky, camera's not right. I think it might be damaged from handling?
messages.raw_text: Hi, I need to file a claim. My iPhone was stolen, then it was found a few days later, but now it's acting up—touchscreen's wonky, camera's not right. I think it might be damaged from handling?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To begin, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To begin, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, the Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8745632100 and the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1. Do I need to give you anything else?
messages.raw_text: Sure, the Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8745632100 and the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1. Do I need to give you anything else?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the specific issues you're experiencing with your iPhone's touchscreen and camera?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the specific issues you're experiencing with your iPhone's touchscreen and camera?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, the touchscreen is unresponsive at times, like it's ghost touching or just won't register my touch. Also, the camera seems like it's not working properly, takes blurry photos sometimes. I never had these problems before.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, the touchscreen is unresponsive at times, like it's ghost touching or just won't register my touch. Also, the camera seems like it's not working properly, takes blurry photos sometimes. I never had these problems before.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I see. Are you experiencing any error messages or unusual behavior when using your iPhone? Also, have you already tried any troubleshooting steps, such as restarting or updating your iOS?
messages.raw_text: I see. Are you experiencing any error messages or unusual behavior when using your iPhone? Also, have you already tried any troubleshooting steps, such as restarting or updating your iOS?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No error messages, just the touchscreen misbehaving and the camera acting weird. I didn't try anything yet, kinda worried if I mess it up further. Just noticing the issues recently after I got it back.
messages.raw_text: No error messages, just the touchscreen misbehaving and the camera acting weird. I didn't try anything yet, kinda worried if I mess it up further. Just noticing the issues recently after I got it back.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. As the issue involves potential damage from handling, could you please tell me where, when, and how the accident occurred? For example, did the device drop, get liquid inside, or suffer any other kind of impact?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. As the issue involves potential damage from handling, could you please tell me where, when, and how the accident occurred? For example, did the device drop, get liquid inside, or suffer any other kind of impact?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, I found it myself after it was stolen and returned, so I honestly don't know exactly how it got damaged. It seemed fine before, and I didn't drop it or get liquid on it. The corrosion near the SIM tray is pretty minor, I think it's just from being handled. I'm worried it might have been damaged in the recovery process or something.
messages.raw_text: Well, I found it myself after it was stolen and returned, so I honestly don't know exactly how it got damaged. It seemed fine before, and I didn't drop it or get liquid on it. The corrosion near the SIM tray is pretty minor, I think it's just from being handled. I'm worried it might have been damaged in the recovery process or something.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for clarifying. To support your claim, would you be able to provide any photos or logs that show the current condition of the device or the corrosion near the SIM tray?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for clarifying. To support your claim, would you be able to provide any photos or logs that show the current condition of the device or the corrosion near the SIM tray?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I can try to take some pictures of the corrosion and the screen, but I’m not sure if they’ll show much. I don’t have any logs or detailed info, just how it looks now. Will that help?
messages.raw_text: I can try to take some pictures of the corrosion and the screen, but I’m not sure if they’ll show much. I don’t have any logs or detailed info, just how it looks now. Will that help?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Yes, photos of the current condition can help validate the circumstances. Please go ahead and send the pictures when you're ready. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we proceed?
messages.raw_text: Yes, photos of the current condition can help validate the circumstances. Please go ahead and send the pictures when you're ready. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we proceed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that's all for now. I'm hoping you'll be able to help me with the claim. Thanks.
messages.raw_text: No, that's all for now. I'm hoping you'll be able to help me with the claim. Thanks.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will now be processed. You will receive a notification as soon as it is complete. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will now be processed. You will receive a notification as soon as it is complete. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day!

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thank you. I appreciate your help. Hope it gets sorted quickly. Goodbye.
messages.raw_text: Thank you. I appreciate your help. Hope it gets sorted quickly. Goodbye.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome! We're working to get your claim processed as quickly as possible. If you need anything else, don't hesitate to reach out. Goodbye!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome! We're working to get your claim processed as quickly as possible. If you need anything else, don't hesitate to reach out. Goodbye! <END>
